name: Vegetable Pullao
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Onion, peeled and finely chopped
4 medium Garlic cloves, peeled and minced
1/3 lb String beans, trimmed, and cut into 1/4 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Put mung beans in a bowl with 3 cups water. Cover lightly and set aside for 12 hours. Drain the beans and wrap them in a very damp dish towel. Place the wrapped bundle in a bowl. Set the bowl in a dark place (like an unused oven) for 24 hours.
2. Wash rice well and soak in 4 cups of water for about 30 minutes. Drain thoroughly.
3. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
4. Heat oil in a wide, heavy, 4- to 5-quart ovenproof pot over medium-high heat. When hot, add mustard seeds. As soon as they begin to pop (this takes just a few seconds), add the onion. Stir and fry for about 5 minutes or until the onion turns brown at the edges.
5. Add garlic and ginger. Fry, stirring, for about 1 minute. Reduce heat to medium-low and add mung beans, rice, string beans, mushrooms, garam masala, ground coriander, and salt. Stir and sauté for about 10 minutes or until the rice turns translucent and vegetables are well coated with oil.
6. Add 4 cups hot water and minced parsley. Increase heat to medium-high and cook, stirring, for about 5 minutes or until most of the water is absorbed (there will be an inch or so of water at the bottom).
7. Cover the pot first with aluminum foil, crimping and sealing the edges, and then with its own lid. Place in the heated oven for half an hour.
8. Fluff up with a fork and serve.
